title: The Search

jokes.text: Michael working on English character 'Reginald Poofter'
jokes.setup: Michael practicing accents on a stranger
jokes.punchline: The absurdly inappropriate character name 'Reginald Poofter'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriateness - the character name is offensive but fits his oblivious personality perfectly

jokes.text: David Brent's racist Chinese character 'Ping' - 'Herro! Herro!'
jokes.setup: Brent trying to one-up Michael with his own character work
jokes.punchline: Blatantly racist impression while claiming it's not about making fun of nationalities
jokes.explanation: Peak Brent behavior - offensive while being completely oblivious to his offensiveness

jokes.text: 'Comedy is a place where the mind goes to tickle itself' / 'That's what she said'
jokes.setup: Brent's pretentious definition of comedy
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate 'That's what she said' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Brent's pseudo-intellectual rambling immediately undercut by Michael's signature catchphrase

jokes.text: 'Master of Ceremonies' Bernard correction
jokes.setup: Someone calls him 'Mr. Bernard'
jokes.punchline: Andy's pretentious correction to 'Master of Ceremonies Bernard'
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy pretentiousness - wants to sound important but comes off as ridiculous

jokes.text: Ryan's timeshare scam story leading to lonely Boise weekend
jokes.setup: Ryan went to learn real estate secrets
jokes.punchline: Got scammed into buying Idaho timeshares and spent lonely weekend in Boise
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from wanting to scam others to being the victim himself, fits Ryan's character arc

jokes.text: Michael's 'Nein, I'm Greek! My name is Mykonos' entrance
jokes.setup: Someone recognizes Michael at the seminar
jokes.punchline: Terrible accent and claiming to be Greek named Mykonos
jokes.explanation: Michael's commitment to obviously fake personas is always funny, 'Nein' followed by 'Greek' is perfect

jokes.text: Mykonos based on Spiros 'who is more about the ladies'
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his character creation process
jokes.punchline: Elaborate backstory for fake Greek persona based on another fake Greek persona
jokes.explanation: Michael's unnecessary over-elaboration of his fake identity is classic character work

jokes.text: 'Thank you so much, Tuna Turner. You are simply the best.'
jokes.setup: Michael talking to Jim
jokes.punchline: Combining 'Tuna' nickname with Tina Turner reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Michael's tendency to make pop culture references and use Jim's nickname

jokes.text: 'I get super flexible when I'm nervous' with Jim stretching
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his nervous habit
jokes.punchline: Visual of Jim doing extreme stretches while talking
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that gives Jim an unexpected quirk

jokes.text: 'If the seminar was a meal, you'd be the amuse-bouche'
jokes.setup: Andy trying to convince Jim to stay for the seminar
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate French culinary metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - unnecessarily pretentious metaphor that actually works but sounds ridiculous

jokes.text: Andy admitting he's terrible at sales and this is the only job he's good at
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why the seminar can't fail
jokes.punchline: Contradictory statement about being bad at sales but good at this job
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy self-defeating logic - admits failure while claiming competence

jokes.text: Abandoned mineshafts business idea - 'big future in that, lots of mines in Scranton'
jokes.setup: Andy asking about business ideas
jokes.punchline: Completely impractical mineshaft business treated as viable
jokes.explanation: Absurd business idea played completely straight, Andy's supportive response makes it funnier

jokes.text: 0.2 cents business model repeated for everything
jokes.setup: Attendee explaining credit card business
jokes.punchline: Same tiny profit model applied to phones, shopping, everything
jokes.explanation: Repetitive escalation works but is fairly basic comedy structure

jokes.text: 'They're baby whales, which is even cuter'
jokes.setup: Dwight complaining about worms instead of whales
jokes.punchline: Andy's spin that baby whales are cuter than regular whales
jokes.explanation: Andy's desperate positive spin is perfectly in character and genuinely clever

jokes.text: 'We're no more a team than people staying in same hotel'
jokes.setup: Andy appealing to team spirit
jokes.punchline: Stanley's perfectly logical dismantling of the team concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures workplace dynamics with Stanley's trademark bluntness

jokes.text: Holly's worst week including mono and first period in high school
jokes.setup: Michael explaining Holly's bad week
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing about Holly's personal history
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael TMI moment, perfectly inappropriate and uncomfortable

jokes.text: 'Too much information? That's what I thought. But here I am using it.'
jokes.setup: Michael acknowledging he overshared
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's TMI but using it anyway
jokes.explanation: Michael's self-awareness paired with complete lack of self-control is perfect character work

jokes.text: 'Mykonos ran away to join Italian circus' backstory
jokes.setup: Someone saying Mykonos sounds Italian
jokes.punchline: Elaborate backstory to explain the accent inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Michael's commitment to building character mythology on the fly is very him

jokes.text: 'Is JLP a word? I Jlp you!'
jokes.setup: Erin struggling with Scrabble
jokes.punchline: Trying to make JLP work as a word with made-up usage
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent attempt to cheat at Scrabble fits her character perfectly

jokes.text: Gabe's horror movie choices vs Erin liking the 'perfect family' parts
jokes.setup: Erin never winning at Scrabble so Gabe picks movies
jokes.punchline: She only likes the beginning parts before the horror starts
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Erin's wholesomeness vs Gabe's dark tastes creates natural comedy

jokes.text: 'It means you're playing someone who's going to destroy you'
jokes.setup: Erin asking what 'ka' means in Scrabble context
jokes.punchline: Oscar's ominous assessment of Gabe's skill level
jokes.explanation: Oscar's intellectual snobbery delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: Erin stuck on cow words - moo, milk, mood, moon - 'cow jumped over the moon'
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to help Erin with Scrabble strategy
jokes.punchline: Erin can only think in cow-related terms for everything
jokes.explanation: Erin's simple thought process creating unintended comedy through limitation

jokes.text: Kevin as Jim in alternate timeline where Jim never met Pam
jokes.setup: Andy saying Kevin could replace Jim
jokes.punchline: Kevin's self-deprecating It's a Wonderful Life reference
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference that's both self-aware and character-appropriate for Kevin

jokes.text: Ryan's commitment paralysis - won't commit to doing or not doing
jokes.setup: Andy asking Ryan to speak at seminar
jokes.punchline: Ryan claiming both yes and no are equal commitments
jokes.explanation: Pure Ryan - pseudo-intellectual reasoning to avoid any responsibility

jokes.text: 'And if I flake, I flake'
jokes.setup: Ryan agreeing to help with seminar
jokes.punchline: Immediately giving himself an out with casual attitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - commits but immediately undermines the commitment

jokes.text: 'My name's Andy Bernard, but you can call me The Nard Dog'
jokes.setup: Andy opening the seminar professionally
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous self-given nickname in business context
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy trying to be cool but coming off as trying too hard

jokes.text: Kevin's speech timing - long awkward pause counting '4, 5'
jokes.setup: Kevin supposed to give inspirational speech
jokes.punchline: Extended uncomfortable silence with visible counting
jokes.explanation: Kevin's public speaking anxiety played for maximum cringe effect

jokes.text: Kevin's motivational speech: 'Dream big... And then double it!'
jokes.setup: Kevin attempting motivational speaking
jokes.punchline: Simplistic advice delivered with unearned confidence
jokes.explanation: Kevin's attempt at profundity coming out as childishly simple

jokes.text: Kevin's winner visualization ending with 'Get out of the way'
jokes.setup: Kevin building to inspirational climax about picturing winners
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical aggressive ending that doesn't follow from setup
jokes.explanation: Kevin's speech devolving into randomness fits his character but lacks strong structure

jokes.text: Kelly crashing Ryan's introduction as 'the business bitch'
jokes.setup: Andy introducing Ryan professionally
jokes.punchline: Kelly barging in with inappropriate self-branding
jokes.explanation: Kelly's inappropriate takeover with terrible personal branding is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Kelly's multiple 'bitch' brands - business, diet, shopping, etiquette
jokes.setup: Kelly introducing herself as business bitch
jokes.punchline: Entire portfolio of 'bitch' branded personas
jokes.explanation: Kelly's misguided attempt at personal branding taken to absurd extremes

jokes.text: Professor on speakerphone: 'Why?' when Kelly explains he's on speaker
jokes.setup: Kelly puts professor on speakerphone for seminar
jokes.punchline: Professor's confused single word response to unexpected situation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective awkwardness from unwitting participant

jokes.text: Professor reluctantly giving business advice: 'know your market... fiscal discipline'
jokes.setup: Kelly pressing professor for the '10 secrets'
jokes.punchline: Generic boring advice delivered reluctantly
jokes.explanation: Anticlimactic payoff to Kelly's buildup, works as deflation but not particularly funny

jokes.text: 'That brilliant little bitch' - Erin about Kelly
jokes.setup: Erin watching Kelly's seminar performance
jokes.punchline: Wholesome Erin using profanity admiringly
jokes.explanation: Erin swearing is always funny, and she's genuinely impressed by Kelly's tactics

jokes.text: Erin's aggressive Scrabble coaching - 'Put the Q there!' 'Sorry I yelled'
jokes.setup: Erin helping with Scrabble strategy
jokes.punchline: Sweet Erin becoming aggressively competitive then immediately apologetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin character moment - competitive streak immediately followed by sweetness

jokes.text: 'I like the musical Grease, or as we call it, home'
jokes.setup: Someone asking Gabe about his movie preferences
jokes.punchline: Gabe identifying completely with Grease as 'home'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Gabe line that reveals his unusual psychology through pop culture reference

jokes.text: 'Greekaccino - very strong coffee with milk from a goat'
jokes.setup: Michael ordering for Holly at coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Made-up drink with absurd Greek-themed description
jokes.explanation: Michael committing to his fake identity with made-up drinks

jokes.text: 'It's a miracle. She can talk. No more brain damage!'
jokes.setup: Holly participating in Michael's bit
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate celebration of fake medical recovery
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate medical humor, cringe but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Creed's Loch Ness Monster business plan speech
jokes.setup: Creed giving business advice to seminar
jokes.punchline: Entire speech about hunting Loch Ness Monster for Scottish riches
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed weirdness - completely unhinged but delivered with total conviction

jokes.text: 'So I have one question, why are you here?'
jokes.setup: Creed's elaborate Loch Ness Monster setup
jokes.punchline: Existential question that has nothing to do with his premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed non-sequitur that somehow works as both absurd and profound

jokes.text: Gabe's horror movie 'Suspiria' vs Erin's 'Wall-E where all boundaries of color are pushed'
jokes.setup: Gabe picking another horror movie for winning
jokes.punchline: Erin's innocent description of Wall-E using Gabe's pretentious language
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Erin adopting Gabe's film pretension for a children's movie

jokes.text: Oscar's Slumdog Millionaire theory about Erin's Scrabble success
jokes.setup: Erin suddenly doing well at Scrabble
jokes.punchline: Elaborate theory connecting her orphan past to word knowledge
jokes.explanation: Oscar's intellectual over-analysis of simple situation fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Real business owner seeking 'manageable growth' advice
jokes.setup: Andy's seminar marketed to small business dreamers
jokes.punchline: Actual successful business owner seeking legitimate advice
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony showing the disconnect between Andy's expectations and reality

jokes.text: Jim's pep talk: 'They're a bunch of jackals... You did this. Bring it home.'
jokes.setup: Andy panicking about closing sales
jokes.punchline: Jim's oddly aggressive motivational language
jokes.explanation: Jim using sales-speak language ironically while actually being supportive

jokes.text: 'I will have apoplexy if you lose. Apoplexy is what I will have.'
jokes.setup: Oscar warning Erin about losing at Scrabble
jokes.punchline: Pretentious word choice repeated for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Oscar's vocabulary showing off paired with unnecessary repetition is perfectly him

jokes.text: 'I played ape' - Erin's winning word
jokes.setup: Big buildup to Erin's final winning word
jokes.punchline: Incredibly simple three-letter word
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - all the buildup for the simplest possible word

jokes.text: Jim's childhood reading group story - blue group vs green group
jokes.setup: Explaining Tom's presence at the seminar
jokes.punchline: Elaborate elementary school social hierarchy based on reading levels
jokes.explanation: Good backstory that explains relationship through childhood academic tracking

jokes.text: 'My mom thinks you're too dumb to hang out with'
jokes.setup: Dwight asking how Jim phrased his mom's suggestion
jokes.punchline: Brutally direct child-level honesty
jokes.explanation: Painful childhood honesty that explains the adult awkwardness

jokes.text: Andy's aggressive closing followed by immediate politeness
jokes.setup: Andy getting aggressive with sales tactics
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching back to polite customer service voice
jokes.explanation: Andy's inability to maintain aggressive persona fits his conflict-averse nature

jokes.text: 'Must be a front for some kind of famous laboratory. 'Cause you're so smart.'
jokes.setup: Tom talking to Jim about his job
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment revealing lingering resentment
jokes.explanation: Adult version of childhood resentment disguised as compliment

jokes.text: 'Where's your jetpack, Zuckerberg?'
jokes.setup: Tom leaving conversation with Jim
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic dig combining tech references
jokes.explanation: Generic smart-person insult using contemporary tech references

jokes.text: 'I guess when you looked in me, you forgot about my balls. They're on the outside.'
jokes.setup: Andy celebrating his sales success
jokes.punchline: Awkward anatomy-based confidence boast
jokes.explanation: Andy trying to be macho but coming off as awkward and anatomically obvious

jokes.text: Michael calling Holly 'Necropolis' and asking for kiss
jokes.setup: Michael celebrating successful day with Holly
jokes.punchline: Romantic moment ruined by death-related pet name
jokes.explanation: Michael's romantic gesture undermined by inappropriate word choice

jokes.text: Gabe's 'Hardware' as Wall-E compromise - 'killer combat robot the government invented to destroy humans'
jokes.setup: Gabe offering movie compromise to Erin
jokes.punchline: Describing violent robot movie as similar to cute Wall-E
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe manipulation - finding the thinnest possible connection to justify his horror preference

jokes.text: 'There's a Shrek 2?' - Erin's amazement
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning Shrek 2
jokes.punchline: Erin's genuine amazement at sequel existing
jokes.explanation: Pure Erin innocence - genuinely excited about mainstream movie sequel

tmdb_overview: An emergency call from Helene forces Jim to abandon Michael at a gas station restroom, and Holly, Erin, and Dwight set out to rescue him. Gabe becomes offended at submissions to caption Pam's latest work of art.
